label: Dallas–Fort Worth

historicalNotes.1970: Strong AM market; country unusually strong for a top-5 DMA; Top 40 powerhouse stations; MOR presence; limited FM penetration (~6 full-power FM vs 10+ AM).
historicalNotes.1985: FM ascendant; country remains strong; album rock established; CHR competition; emerging FM fragmentation.
historicalNotes.2000: Large Spanish presence; consolidation era; strong clusters; multiple successful spoken-word formats.
historicalNotes.2020: Highly fragmented; major country presence remains; significant Spanish ecosystem; strong spoken-word footprint; ~38 measurable commercial signals.

selectBlurb: A booming Texas Sunbelt metro where country heritage runs deeper than most large markets, AM news/talk/sports big sticks ruled the 1970s dial, and Hispanic FM growth reshapes the book from the 1990s onward.
